Description: Column with children list computes all items upfront; ListView.builder uses lazy rendering building only visible items plus a small buffer
Do: Use ListView.builder with itemCount and itemBuilder; set itemExtent if all items same height for maximum performance; cacheExtent for look-ahead
Don't: Wrap long lists in SingleChildScrollView + Column (OOM risk with hundreds of items)
Code Good: ListView.builder(itemCount: items.length, itemBuilder: (ctx, i) => ItemRow(item: items[i]), itemExtent: 72)
Code Bad: Column(children: items.map((i) => ItemRow(item: i)).toList())  // All items built at once

Guideline: Use const constructors to prevent unnecessary rebuilds
Description: Widgets without const rebuild even if their props haven't changed; const widgets are compile-time constants and never rebuilt
Do: Add const to all widgets possible: const Text() const Icon() const SizedBox() const Padding(); use const everywhere in widget trees
Don't: Build widget trees without const; create new Color/TextStyle instances in build() without const
Code Good: Column(children: [const Text('Hello'), const Icon(Icons.star), const SizedBox(height: 16)])
Code Bad: Column(children: [Text('Hello'), Icon(Icons.star), SizedBox(height: 16)])  // Rebuilt on every parent rebuild

Description: Everything in build() runs on every rebuild triggered by setState or parent rebuild; expensive operations slow frames
Do: Move expensive computations outside build(); use cached_network_image for images; define static data as class constants; use RepaintBoundary to isolate subtrees
Don't: Parse JSON/dates in build(); make network calls in build(); create complex data structures in build()
Code Good: class _MyState extends State<My> { late final sorted = items.sorted(); @override Widget build(ctx) => ListView.builder(itemCount: sorted.length) }
Code Bad: Widget build(ctx) => ListView.builder(itemCount: items.sorted().length)  // sorted() runs every frame

Guideline: Use GoRouter for type-safe declarative routing
Description: Navigator 1.0 push/pop is imperative and doesn't support deep links/web well; Navigator 2.0 is complex; GoRouter provides declarative URL-based routing with type-safety
Do: Define routes in GoRouter with path patterns; use go() not push() for replacement; goNamed() for type-safe navigation; ShellRoute for persistent bottom nav
Don't: Use Navigator.push() for deep-link-compatible screens; use route names as plain strings

Guideline: Configure bottom tab persistence with StatefulShellRoute
Description: Simple BottomNavigationBar with Navigator loses stack state when switching tabs; StatefulShellRoute preserves each branch's navigation stack
Do: Use StatefulShellRoute.indexedStack() in GoRouter; each branch gets its own Navigator; state preserved on tab switch
Don't: Replace tabs by popping all routes on switch; use separate NavigatorKeys manually without GoRouter
Code Bad: BottomNavigationBar(onTap:(i)=>setState(()=>_index=i)) // Loses stack state on tab switch

Guideline: Use SafeArea widget for notch-safe layouts
Description: Widgets directly in Scaffold body without SafeArea can render behind notch status bar or home indicator
Do: Wrap content in SafeArea; Scaffold already handles top nav bar area but body content needs SafeArea for edge cases; pass minimum parameter for partial areas
Don't: Hardcode top padding; use MediaQuery.of(ctx).padding.top directly without SafeArea widget
Code Bad: Scaffold(body: Column(children: [Padding(padding: EdgeInsets.only(top: 44), child: YourContent())]))  // Hardcoded

Guideline: Use MediaQuery and LayoutBuilder for responsive layouts
Description: Hardcoded dimensions break on different screen sizes; responsive layouts adapt to available space
Do: Use MediaQuery.sizeOf(context) for screen dimensions; LayoutBuilder for parent-constrained sizing; avoid accessing in build root (use Slivers instead)
Don't: Hardcode pixel width for phone (assumes same screen); use MediaQuery deep in widget tree frequently (causes rebuild cascade)
Code Bad: Container(width: 375)  // Breaks on any other screen size

Description: Building custom scaffold-like structures loses MaterialApp integration (Drawer BottomSheet Snackbar FAB positioning) and visual regression risks
Do: Use Scaffold for every screen; use Scaffold's body bottomNavigationBar floatingActionButton drawer appBar properties; nest Scaffolds only for specific cases
Don't: Create custom Stack-based layouts to replicate Scaffold behavior; use root-level Stack for FAB and bottom bar
Code Good: Scaffold(appBar: AppBar(title: Text('Home')), body: HomeContent(), bottomNavigationBar: NavBar(), floatingActionButton: FAB())

Guideline: Use Riverpod for scalable state management
Description: StatefulWidget setState works for local state but global/shared state requires proper DI; Provider is deprecated in favor of Riverpod
Do: Use Riverpod: StateNotifierProvider for mutable state; FutureProvider for async; ref.watch in build; ref.read in callbacks
Don't: Use setState for data shared across screens; use Provider package (deprecated); global variables for state
Code Good: @riverpod class CounterNotifier extends _$CounterNotifier { @override int build() => 0; void increment() => state++; } // Widget: ref.watch(counterNotifierProvider)
Code Bad: class _State extends State<Widget> { int counter = 0; void inc() => setState(()=>counter++); } // Not scalable for shared state

Guideline: Use Semantics widget to provide accessible labels
Description: Many custom widgets have no semantic meaning by default; users of TalkBack and screen readers cannot understand the interface
Do: Wrap custom interactive widgets in Semantics; use semanticsLabel for Images; excludeFromSemantics for decorative elements; mergeAllDescendantsIntoTree for grouped items
Don't: Ignore Semantics for custom widgets; use Icon without semanticLabel; forget to mark headings
Code Good: Semantics(label: 'Profile photo of ${user.name}', image: true, child: CircleAvatar(backgroundImage: NetworkImage(user.avatarUrl)))

Guideline: Support font scaling with sp-equivalent sizing
Description: Flutter Text uses logical pixels by default; users who increase system font size in Android/iOS settings see no change
Do: Use MediaQuery.textScalerOf or textScaleFactor in views; do not use textScaleFactor:1.0 to force disable; allow text to grow
Don't: Disable text scaling globally with textScaleFactor:1.0; clamp at small scale; overflow text at large scale using fixed containers
Code Good: Text('Hello', style: TextStyle(fontSize: 16))  // font scales automatically in Flutter by default — just dont disable it
Code Bad: Text('Hello', textScaleFactor: 1.0, style: TextStyle(fontSize: 16))  // Disables user font size preference

Description: NetworkImage and Image.network don't cache to disk; same URL re-downloads on every screen visit causing slow UX and excess bandwidth
Do: Use CachedNetworkImage; include placeholder and errorWidget; use cacheKey for URL params that change but image doesn't
Don't: Use Image.network for any image that may appear more than once; fetch same URLs repeatedly without cache
Code Good: CachedNetworkImage(imageUrl: url, placeholder: (ctx,url)=>Shimmer(), errorWidget: (ctx,url,err)=>Icon(Icons.error), fit: BoxFit.cover)
Code Bad: Image.network(url, fit: BoxFit.cover)  // No caching, re-downloads every time

Guideline: Use Hero widget for shared element transitions
Description: Hero provides automatic shared element transition between routes for a specific tagged widget making transitions feel native and connected
Do: Add Hero widget with same tag string to same element on both source and destination screens; tag must be unique per screen
Don't: Use Hero with non-unique tags (shows debug error); Hero on lists with all items same tag
Code Good: // Source screen: Hero(tag: 'product-${item.id}', child: Image(url)); // Destination: Hero(tag: 'product-${item.id}', child: LargeImage(url))
Code Bad: Hero(tag: 'product', child: Image(url))  // Non-unique tag — breaks when multiple items on screen

Description: Animation setup with AnimationController requires dispose() to prevent memory leaks; AnimatedBuilder rebuilds less than setState
Do: Use SingleTickerProviderStateMixin; dispose controller in dispose(); use AnimatedBuilder to limit rebuilds to animated subtree
Don't: Forget to dispose AnimationController (memory leak); call setState on every animation tick (rebuilds entire tree)
Code Good: class _MyState extends State<My> with SingleTickerProviderStateMixin { late final _ctrl = AnimationController(vsync:this, duration:300.ms); @override void dispose() { _ctrl.dispose(); super.dispose(); } }
Code Bad: class _MyState extends State<My> { final ctrl = AnimationController(duration:300.ms); } // No vsync no dispose

Guideline: Handle image loading states and errors
Description: Images that fail to load or take time show empty/broken UI; no placeholder = layout shift on load
Do: Always provide placeholder and error widgets; use FadeInImage for smooth appearance; handle loading shimmer
Don't: Hope images always load instantly; skip errorWidget; use Image without placeholder causing layout shift
Code Good: FadeInImage(placeholder: MemoryImage(kTransparentImage), image: NetworkImage(url), fit: BoxFit.cover, fadeInDuration: 200.ms)
Code Bad: Image.network(url)  // No placeholder, no error, layout shift on load

Guideline: Use Platform.isIOS and Platform.isAndroid for platform logic
Description: UI patterns differ between iOS and Android; platform checks allow delivering native-appropriate experience
Do: Use Platform.isIOS in dart:io for runtime; use defaultTargetPlatform for widget-level; use Theme.of() to access Material/Cupertino theme
Don't: Force iOS visual style on Android or vice versa; use .platform == 'ios' string comparison

Guideline: Use Flutter widget testing for UI verification
Description: Integration tests are slow and flaky; widget tests run in memory and verify widget tree behavior without device
Do: Use WidgetTester.pumpWidget() pump() pumpAndSettle(); find by semantics label (find.bySemanticsLabel) or type; test user interactions with tester.tap()
Don't: Only test business logic ignoring UI; use only integration tests; test by index (find.at(0)) which breaks on reorder
Code Good: testWidgets('shows user name', (tester) async { await tester.pumpWidget(app); await tester.pump(); expect(find.bySemanticsLabel('Alice'), findsOneWidget); })
Code Bad: testWidgets('test', (t) async { await t.pumpWidget(app); expect(find.byType(Text).at(0), findsOneWidget); })  // Fragile index-based

Guideline: Use AsyncValue in Riverpod for loading/error states
Description: FutureProvider returns raw Future without loading and error states; AsyncValue provides all three states in one pattern
Do: Use FutureProvider; handle AsyncValue.when(data:,loading:,error:) in UI; never assume data is ready without check
Don't: Access .value directly on AsyncValue without null check; show nothing for loading state
Code Bad: final posts = ref.watch(postsProvider).value!  // Throws if loading or error
